scenario_results.name: Campus core redesign intake and architecture recommendation
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario requires authoritative topology, IPAM, STP, OSPF, change, monitoring, and rollback records before accepted guidance.
validated_outputs: architecture recommendations with explicit trade-offs and operational consequences

scenario_results.name: BGP policy and route-leak prevention review
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario uses BGP neighbor, accepted/advertised route, route policy, flap, and rollback evidence.
validated_outputs: configuration correction plan with guardrails and risk posture

scenario_results.name: OSPF area boundary and summarization correction
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario validates OSPF area, LSA, adjacency, CPU, topology, maintenance-window, and rollback evidence requirements.
validated_outputs: configuration correction plan with staged sequence

scenario_results.name: STP root drift after switch replacement
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario uses VLAN, bridge priority, port-role, MAC movement, and link-state records to constrain triage.
validated_outputs: triage report with escalation points and confidence levels

scenario_results.name: LACP port-channel instability
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario covers interface counters, LACP actor/partner state, platform state, logs, and vendor/human escalation.
validated_outputs: low-confidence or vendor-escalation note when required

scenario_results.name: VRRP and BFD convergence tradeoff
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario validates gateway redundancy, BFD, timer, link, service-criticality, and recovery-objective evidence.

scenario_results.name: Asymmetric routing under dual-homed paths
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario splits routing/switching evidence from firewall/perimeter ownership when stateful inspection is implicated.

scenario_results.name: VLAN migration with DHCP relay dependency
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario validates trunk, SVI, relay, IPAM, DHCP, and DNS evidence while preserving DNS/DHCP/IPAM handoff boundaries.

scenario_results.name: Missing prerequisite information before acceptance
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario blocks substantive guidance until topology, configuration, telemetry, change, authority, and policy facts are present.

scenario_results.name: Invalid or ineligible request
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario refuses production execution and final approval when authorization, rollback owner, and change record are absent.

scenario_results.name: Case that must be routed elsewhere before execution begins
families: request requiring a more specialized adjacent lane
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario routes firewall, VPN, wireless, and endpoint-owned requests before execution while preserving evidence context.

scenario_results.name: Endpoint and identity boundary refusal
families: request requiring a more specialized adjacent lane
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario explicitly rejects endpoint administration, OS patching, and identity/authentication design ownership.

scenario_results.name: Application-only architecture boundary
families: boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario rejects application-only architecture, coding, and app-level performance tuning without network evidence.

scenario_results.name: Firewall, IDS, anti-malware, and IAM command refusal
families: request requiring a more specialized adjacent lane
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario refuses firewall, IDS, endpoint anti-malware, and IAM operational commands and routes to security/endpoint/identity lanes.

scenario_results.name: Legal, audit, procurement, and approval refusal
families: boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario refuses compliance attestation, vendor selection, budget approval, risk acceptance, and legal authority.

scenario_results.name: Low-confidence escalation with contradictory records
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario escalates when topology, config repository, hostname, OSPF, and route table records conflict.

scenario_results.name: Cross-industry critical-service and safety facts missing
families: industry-specific authority or safety facts missing
industry name materially changes the pack
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario requires sector-specific safety, regulated-service, policy, and approval facts before shortening convergence timers.

scenario_results.name: Tenant-specific adaptation with no unreviewed regressions
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario records public baseline, tenant evidence, source precedence, assumptions, and regression review before validated claim.

notes: Executed 18 spec-pack scenarios covering routing/switching design, configuration review, fault isolation, performance/availability trade-offs, edge-case failures, intake prerequisites, boundary rejection, low-confidence escalation, cross-industry safety facts, tenant adaptation, and regression review.
All scenarios passed explicit acceptance criteria with no unreviewed regressions.
Functionality coverage is complete for six shared abilities, three intake/access/initiation abilities, and the cross-industry artifact-awareness ability.
The refreshed pack remains specification-based and does not claim retained implementation ownership.
